title: Is a Lower Combined Ratio Key to ROOT's Profitability?
text: For 2025, Root's combined ratio finished at 98.2 versus 96.4 in 2024. In Q4 2025, Root Inc. booked a combined ratio of 99.7 compared with 91.5 for Q4 2024. The company's net loss and LAE ratio was 65.9%, and its net combined ratio was 98.2%, indicating essentially breakeven underwriting after expenses with positive overall earnings. A combined ratio below 100% signals underwriting profitability. Root's management has indicated that maintaining profitability depends on keeping the combined ratio below 100%. With the Federal Reserve's recent rate cut and anticipated further easing putting additional pressure on yields, sustained profitability depends on keeping the combined ratio below 100%. Root has shown significant improvement over time. Its net combined ratio improved to 94.2% in 2024, reflecting a 3,680-basis-point year-over-year gain, and strengthened further in the first half of 2025 with a significant 700-basis-point improvement. The company's focus on technology and data-driven pricing has been key to their improvement strategy, with management emphasizing the importance of achieving and maintaining underwriting profitability while growing their policy base.
